title: High rise lateral system comparison
description_for_llm: Linear elastic (Theory I) analysis of a tall building wireframe comparing lateral systems (frame, braced, tube style member layouts) under gravity and a horizontal load. Use for early massing studies of towers where relative drift and member utilization between system options matter more than absolute values. Assumptions: the tower is a line element wireframe in metres with rigid floor to column joints, supports fixed at ground nodes, simplified equivalent static horizontal load, one steel section family. Limits: no floor diaphragms or core walls as shells, so torsional behaviour is crude; no second order effects, which matter for tall slender towers; no code level wind or seismic load derivation; foundation flexibility ignored. Trust trends between options, not absolute drift numbers. NOT YET AUDITED: the GHX has no ALMOND_IN_*/ALMOND_OUT_* harness params, so run_definition will refuse it. Retrieval context only until harnessed.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_LINES
inputs.description: Tower member axis curves (columns, beams, bracing), node-merged at joints.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_LOAD_KN
inputs.description: Total equivalent static horizontal load distributed over floor levels.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_DISP_MM
outputs.description: Largest nodal displacement, normally the roof drift.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_UTIL
outputs.description: Utilization ratio per element, 1.0 means at capacity.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_MASS_KG
outputs.description: Total structural mass of the model.
